Digital Signage Installation Services in Singapore — LED Screens & Video Walls

Our certified engineers ensure safe and secure installation of all display types. We handle structural mounting, cabling, electrical integration, and system calibration to ensure your displays look perfect and operate safely.

Professional implementation is the difference between a display that simply “works” and a digital signage system that performs reliably, looks seamless, and keeps people safe.

Professional Implementation & Engineering

Your digital signage investment deserves installation that matches its quality. Auronova’s Installation Services are led by certified engineers and experienced technicians who understand both the technical and practical requirements of deploying LED screens, LCD panels, interactive displays, video walls, and kiosks in real-world environments. We don’t just put screens on walls; we engineer complete, safe, and robust systems that are ready for daily use in demanding locations.

From structural mounting and cabling to electrical integration and final system calibration, every step is carefully planned and executed. Our goal is to ensure that your displays look perfect, operate safely, and continue to perform reliably long after the installation team has left your site.

End-to-End Installation by Certified Digital Signage Engineers

Digital signage installations often involve more than just hanging a display. There are structural, electrical, and safety considerations that must be handled correctly from day one. Auronova’s certified engineers take full responsibility for the technical integrity of your installation, working closely with your internal teams and other contractors on site.

Our installation process typically includes:

Pre-installation site surveys covering environment, mounting surfaces, power and cable routes

Review of architectural drawings and coordination with building management and contractors

Selection of appropriate mounting structures, brackets and enclosures for each display type

Planning of power and data distribution for a reliable, compliant installation

This engineering-led approach ensures that your digital signage network is not only visually impressive but also structurally sound and future-ready.

Professional Installation for All Digital Signage Display Types

Every installation is different. A lightweight display in a small meeting room has very different requirements from a large-format LED wall in a busy atrium. Auronova’s team is trained to handle all major display types, including:

Indoor LED cabinets and video walls

Outdoor and semi-outdoor LED panels with weather-resistant housing

High-brightness shopfront displays for street-facing retail

Interactive kiosks and freestanding totems in public spaces

Wall-mounted screens for offices, classrooms and corridors

For each project, we calculate load-bearing requirements, choose mounting systems rated for the specific display and environment, and secure fixtures using appropriate anchors and fasteners. We pay special attention to high-traffic and public areas, where safety and durability are critical. The result is a secure installation you can trust, even under constant daily use.

Structural Mounting for Safe, Secure Digital Signage Installations

Solid structural mounting is the foundation of a reliable display system. Poorly mounted screens can tilt, vibrate, or, in the worst cases, become safety hazards. Auronova treats mounting as a critical engineering task, not an afterthought.

Our team:

Structural assessment of walls, ceilings and pillars to confirm load-bearing capacity

Selection of wall brackets, ceiling mounts or custom frames suited to display size and substrate

Level, aligned positioning at the correct height and viewing angles for your audience

Custom frame design and installation for video walls with clean, consistent panel gaps

We also consider access for future maintenance: where possible, mounts are chosen to allow for service access without having to dismantle entire structures, saving time and reducing disruption later.

Clean, Organised Cabling for Every Digital Signage Installation

Behind every clean-looking digital signage installation is a carefully managed cabling plan. Messy cables are more than an eyesore—they increase the risk of faults, complicate maintenance, and can even create safety issues.

Auronova’s installation services include:

Structured cable routes for power, signal and network connections

Conduits, trunking and cable trays to keep cabling protected, hidden and organised

Labelled cables and connection points for easier troubleshooting and future upgrades

Separated power and data lines to minimise interference and maintain signal quality

We aim to make every installation as neat behind the scenes as it is in front. This attention to detail pays off when you need to expand your system, troubleshoot an issue, or simply keep your spaces looking tidy and professional.

Electrical Integration for Digital Signage Systems

Digital signage depends on stable, well-designed electrical infrastructure. Under-sized circuits, poorly placed outlets, or ad hoc connections can lead to failures, flickering, or shutdowns at the worst possible time. Auronova’s team ensures that each display has the power it needs, delivered safely and reliably.

Our electrical integration covers:

Assessing existing electrical capacity and advising on any upgrades required

Positioning power points and circuits to match display locations and mounting plans

Working with licensed electricians to ensure compliance with local regulations

Surge protection and power conditioning to protect sensitive display equipment

By treating electrical integration as a core part of the installation, we help you avoid hidden risks and downtime, and extend the lifespan of your displays and media players.

System Calibration for Accurate, Consistent Visuals

Even the best hardware will not look its best without proper calibration. After installation, Auronova performs system calibration so that each display delivers optimal colour, brightness, and clarity for its environment.

Calibration may include:

Adjusting brightness and contrast to match ambient light levels without glare

Fine-tuning colour balance so brand colours appear consistent across multiple screens

Aligning resolutions and aspect ratios for sharp, correctly framed content

Synchronising multi-screen and video wall setups for seamless panel alignment

For interactive displays, we also verify touch responsiveness and alignment, ensuring that touchpoints match what users see on screen. This final tuning step helps your content look exactly as intended and supports a premium visual experience.

Minimising Disruption to Your Business During Installation

We understand that many installations take place in active environments—retail outlets, offices, schools, clinics, and public spaces where you cannot simply “shut down” for a long period. Auronova plans installations to minimise disruption to your daily operations.

This can include:

Scheduling on-site work outside peak business hours or in phased stages

Coordinating with your internal teams so affected areas are prepared and safe

Temporary safety barriers and signage where required

Full clean-up on completion, leaving your space ready for normal operations

Our teams work efficiently and professionally, respecting your premises and the people who use them, so you can carry on with business while the upgrade takes shape around you.

Stakeholder Coordination for Complex Digital Signage Projects

Digital signage installations often overlap with other ongoing works—fit-outs, renovations, interior design, IT infrastructure, or event setups. Auronova has extensive experience coordinating with different stakeholders to keep projects on track.

We can work alongside:

Architects and interior designers — ensuring displays integrate with your space design

Main contractors — aligning timelines and sharing structural and electrical works information

IT teams — integrating displays into your network for secure, stable content delivery

Marketing and brand teams — confirming screen placement supports content strategies

This collaborative approach helps avoid clashes and rework, saving time and cost while ensuring a smooth path to go-live.

Quality Assurance and Handover Process

Before we hand over any installation, Auronova runs through a detailed quality assurance checklist. This ensures that everything is correctly installed, safely secured, and functioning as expected.

During QA and handover, we:

Testing each display for power-up, input sources and content playback

Verifying scheduled content and network connectivity at every screen

Checking all mounts, brackets and enclosures for stability and alignment

Full system walkthrough with your team covering key components and access points

We also provide basic training so your team understands how to operate the system day to day, and how to perform simple checks before calling for support. Clear documentation on configuration and wiring is prepared where appropriate, laying a solid foundation for future maintenance or expansion.

Installed for Long-Term Maintainability

A well-installed system is easier and more cost-effective to maintain. Auronova’s installation philosophy always considers long-term maintenance and support needs from the outset.

By choosing accessible mounting solutions, organised cabling, and thoughtful placement of equipment, we:

Reducing time required for future inspections and repairs

Lowering the risk of accidental damage when panels need to be removed or serviced

Making it easier to swap components or upgrade hardware as technology evolves

This forward-looking approach delivers better total value over the lifespan of your digital signage investment, not just on installation day.

Why Choose Auronova for Digital Signage Installation in Singapore?

When you choose Auronova, you are choosing a team that combines engineering discipline with practical experience in digital signage deployments. You get:

Certified engineers with full understanding of display installation technical and safety requirements

Full-service approach covering structural mounting, cabling, electrical integration and calibration

Careful project planning that minimises disruption and coordinates all stakeholders

Installations built to look clean, perform reliably and support long-term maintenance

Your screens are the visible face of your digital signage strategy. Our job is to make sure that everything behind them—mounts, cables, power, and calibration—is as professional as what your customers see on the front.

If you’re ready to deploy new displays or upgrade existing ones, Auronova’s Professional Implementation & Engineering services will ensure your installation is safe, secure, and ready to perform from day one.

Frequently Asked Questions — Digital Signage Installation Services

How long does digital signage installation take?

A single wall-mounted display can usually be installed and calibrated in a few hours. A multi-panel video wall takes longer, often somewhere between a day and a few days. Bigger or more complex jobs, such as outdoor LED façades, rollouts across multiple sites, or anything that needs structural work and electrical upgrades, naturally take more time and are usually done in stages. After we’ve surveyed your site, we’ll give you a clear timeline, and where it helps, we can schedule the work outside your busy hours so your day-to-day operations aren’t affected.

How much does it cost to install digital signage in Singapore?

Installation cost comes down to the type and size of the display, how complex the mounting is, the cabling distance, any electrical work needed, and site conditions like ceiling height or hoarding requirements. A straightforward single-screen wall mount costs a good deal less than a video wall, which needs custom framing, panel alignment, and calibration, or an outdoor setup that requires weatherproof housing. We provide an itemised quotation after the site survey, so you’ll know exactly what’s included before any work starts.

Do I need professional installation for digital signage?

For any commercial setup, we’d strongly recommend it. Professional installation means the load-bearing is properly assessed, the screen is securely mounted, the electrical work is compliant, the cabling is tidy, and everything is correctly calibrated. All of that affects safety, reliability, and how good your display looks. A poorly mounted screen can tilt, vibrate, or even become a hazard in a public space, and rushed wiring tends to lead to faults and downtime later. Our certified engineers handle the whole process for you, from the site survey through to QA testing and handover.

Can I use a normal TV for digital signage?

You can, but it isn’t built for the job. Consumer TVs aren’t designed to run continuously the way commercial displays are. Commercial-grade screens are brighter, manage heat better, come with longer warranties, and are rated for 16/7 or even 24/7 operation. A regular TV left running all day in a bright shop will dim, overheat, or fail much sooner than you’d hope. For shopfronts, public areas, and anything that needs to stay on, a commercial display or LED panel is far more reliable and works out cheaper over time. We’re happy to advise on what suits your space.

What is required before installing digital signage?

A few things need to be in place. You’ll want a mounting surface that can safely carry the weight, power points on suitably rated circuits, network connectivity (wired or wireless) for delivering content, and enough clearance for ventilation and future servicing. If you’re in a tenanted space like a mall, you’ll often need approval from the landlord or building management, and some external signage may need authority approvals too. Our pre-installation site survey checks all of this, and we coordinate with building management and other contractors on your behalf so nothing gets missed.

Will installation disrupt my business operations?

With good planning, very little. We schedule the work outside your peak hours or break it into phases, set up safety barriers and signage around the work area, brief your team in advance, and clean everything up once we’re done. Most retail and office installations are completed without you ever having to close, so you can keep serving customers while the new setup goes in around you.
